text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little village nestled between whispering woods and twinkling rivers, there lived a boy named Oliver who had a peculiar hobby—he collected forgotten legends. He'd spend hours wandering the village corners, listening to old women who loved nothing more than sharing tales.

One crisp autumn day, as the chill hinted at winter, Oliver stumbled upon an old ruin by the river. It seemed untouched for centuries, its stones worn smooth by water. Curiosity got the better of him. He climbed atop the rubble to find a cavity behind a collapsed archway. Pulling aside the debris, he discovered a leather-bound book, the cover missing a little feather. His fingers danced across its cover as he read aloud,

"In the heart of the forgotten woods, where the night swallows the sun, lays a stone hearth that never warms. Within its cold embrace lies the Whispering Willows Book—a tale so potent, it could change your destiny."
